exports.name: readGame
exports.signature: (gameId: string, onChange: (game: GameDocument) -> void) -> Unsubscribe; subscribes to live updates of the game document and invokes onChange on every change

exports.name: submitHandful
exports.signature: (gameId: string, roundIndex: integer, submissions: PlayerSubmission[]) -> Promise<{success: bool, status: 'waiting_for_opponent' | 'round_complete' | 'game_complete'}>; idempotent: re-calling with the same (gameId, roundIndex) and the same UID overwrites the prior write rather than duplicating

invariants: Section-4 receives the GameDocument shape exclusively through readGame; it never queries Firestore directly.

Section-4 enforces UI-side answer blindness by only rendering opponent submissions when the GameDocument's round status indicates both players have submitted that round (the adapter is permitted to return a redacted GameDocument view in which opponent submissions are masked until both have submitted, as a defense-in-depth layer behind the Security Rules).
submitHandful idempotency means section-4 can retry on transient network errors safely.
